fact: July 2 is exactly 183 days into the year - that's almost perfectly halfway through! In leap years, it's day 184, so you're officially past the middle.
Baby deer called fawns are wobbling around on their skinny legs right now! Most are only a few weeks old and still have those adorable white spots.
The sun rises before 5:30 AM in many northern places like Canada and Alaska - that's earlier than most kids wake up for school!
Fireflies are having their biggest light shows of the year! They blink in special patterns to find mates, like nature's own disco party.
In Australia and other southern places, this is the dead of winter - some areas might even have snow while the north is sweltering!
Wild strawberries are ripening in forests across North America and Europe, turning from white to bright red and super sweet.
The Cancer zodiac sign rules this date, which means the moon has extra influence - tides can be especially dramatic near new or full moons!
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying peak right now in many places because the warm, humid weather is perfect for their gross breeding cycle.
Sunsets happen after 8 PM in northern cities like Seattle and London - that's way past some bedtimes but perfect for late evening adventures!
Tree leaves are working overtime! They're at maximum green because chlorophyll is pumping hard to capture all that intense summer sunlight.
Ocean water temperatures hit their stride around now - not the warmest yet, but perfect for swimming without freezing your toes off!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest songs during the hottest part of summer days, and males can buzz louder than a lawn mower!
